title: Astragalus lang-ranii
intro: Astragalus lang-ranii, also known as Langran's milkvetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may aid in reducing stress and improving overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus lang-ranii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Astragalus lang-ranii is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared in the form of dried roots or extracts, as well as in capsules or teas, to leverage its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Astragalus lang-ranii is classified under the family Fabaceae and the genus Astragalus. It is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family. The genus Astragalus contains a large number of species, many of which are found in Asia. Astragalus lang-ranii is a rare species that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The classification of Astragalus lang-ranii is further detailed in the botanical and systematic classification of the plant kingdom.
uses: Astragalus lang-ranii is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in China. Its medicinal uses include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is also believed to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune system. side_effects: Astragalus lang-ranii is a plant that has been traditionally used in medicine, but its side effects are not well-documented. However, similar to other Astragalus species, Astragalus lang-ranii may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and diarrhea. It may also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners, diabetes medications, and immunosuppressants.
